description: The scene opens on the exterior of a multi-story brick apartment building under a cloudy sky. The setting then changes to a kitchen, where Chandler and Joey sit at a counter, wearing robes.
Joey eats cereal and says, "Man, this is weird. Ever realize Captain Crunch's eyebrows are actually on his hat?"
Chandler points his spoon at Joey and says, "That's what's weird. Joey, the man's been captain of a cereal for the last 40 years." Joey continues to eat.
Joey opens a drawer, pulls out a spoon, licks it, wipes it on a towel, and places it back in the drawer. Chandler watches, his mouth falling open as audience laughter is heard.
Chandler points at the drawer and says, "Oh, look." Joey asks, "What?"
Chandler keeps his hand extended toward the drawer.
"The spoon," Chandler says.
Chandler gestures forward, saying, "You licked and you put." Audience laughter sounds.
Joey stares at Chandler with his mouth slightly open.
"You licked and you put," Chandler repeats, gesturing with both hands over audience laughter.
Joey remains still, looking at Chandler.
Joey says, "Yeah, so?" Chandler leans forward, gesturing, and replies, "Well, don't you see how gross that is? I mean, that's like you're using my toothbrush."
Joey nods slightly, looking down, while audience laughter plays.
Joey points at Chandler and says, "You use my toothbrush."
Chandler's eyes widen, and he points back at Joey.
Joey gestures with one hand and says, "That was only because I used the red one to unclog the drain." Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler stands up abruptly, his hands rising to his sides.
"Mine is the red one," Chandler says, prompting audience laughter.
Chandler's mouth hangs open as he stares at Joey.
Chandler flails his arms upward and shouts, "Oh, God. Can open. Worms everywhere," his volume rising over audience laughter.
Joey gestures with his hands, remaining seated.
"Hey, why can't we use the same toothbrush but we can use the same soap?" Joey asks, extending his hands. Audience laughter sounds briefly.
Chandler places his hands on his hips and replies, "Because soap is soap."
Chandler maintains his posture, his gaze fixed on Joey.
Chandler extends one hand and says, "It's self cleaning." Audience laughter plays.
Joey nods, slightly smiling.
Joey wags his finger and says, "All right. Well, next time you take a shower think about the last thing I wash and the first thing you wash." Audience laughter builds.
Chandler's expression falls into a sustained stare, his hands clasped together near his waist, as loud audience laughter continues.
The screen goes black and remains black in silence.
The scene brightens to show Joey entering another apartment, wearing a sweater. Chandler follows him inside.
"Hey," Joey says.
Monica, Phoebe, and Chandler are seated around a kitchen table.
"Look at you fancy lads," Phoebe says, holding a mug. Audience laughter is heard.
Joey approaches the table.
"What's the occasion?" Phoebe asks.
Joey gestures and says, "Well, you know the guy on my show that's in a coma?" Audience laughter plays.
Joey stands near the table, looking at Phoebe.
"He's having a brunch," Joey adds.
Rachel walks into the kitchen from the hallway.
Rachel says, "Hey. Ready when you are." Phoebe stands up and says, "Okie dokie." Chandler claps his hands together and says, "Can't believe you guys are actually getting tattoos."
Joey points at them and asks, "Excuse me, guys are getting tattoos?"
Rachel pours juice from a pitcher and says, "Yes, but you cannot tell Ross, because I want to surprise him."
Joey sits down and asks, "Wow, this is wild. What are you going to get?" Phoebe says, "I'm getting"
"A lily," Phoebe finishes, opening a bottle of milk.
Rachel takes a sip from her glass of juice.
"From my mom because her name is Lily," Phoebe continues, pointing to her chest while holding a mug. Audience laughter sounds.
Chandler lowers his glass of juice.
"Wow, that's lucky. What if her name was Big Ugly Splotch?" Chandler says, holding his glass as audience laughter is heard.
Joey looks up at Phoebe.
Joey asks, "So where are you getting it?" Phoebe points to her shoulder and says, "I think on my shoulder." Ross walks through the front door and asks, "What?"
"What's on your shoulder?" Ross asks, taking off his jacket.
Phoebe raises her hand.
"A chip," Phoebe says, prompting audience laughter.
Ross finishes taking off his jacket, looking at Phoebe.
"I've got a big attitude problem," Phoebe says, waving her hands. Audience laughter plays.
Ross walks toward the kitchen table, his brow furrowed.
"A tattoo," Rachel says.
Rachel looks steadily at Ross.
"I'm getting a tattoo," Rachel adds.
"A tattoo? Why?" Ross says, stopping near the table.
"Why would you want to do that?" Ross continues. Rachel steps closer to him, placing her hands on his chest, and says softly, "Hi." Audience laughter sounds.
Ross looks down at Rachel.
"Well, hey," Ross says. Rachel asks, "You don't think they're kind of cool?"
"No. Sorry, I don't," Ross replies, shaking his head.
"I mean, why would anyone pay someone to scar their body for life?" Ross asks, gesturing. Rachel drops her hands from his chest.
"And what if it doesn't come out right, phoebes. And it's" Ross continues.
"Like, I don't know, having a bad haircut all the time?" Ross finishes, raising his arms. Audience laughter is heard.
Ross looks around the table; the others are silently staring at him.
"Why is everyone staring at me?" Ross asks, his arms returning to his sides.
Joey glances down, holding a yellow mug.

Monica says, "Ross, come sign this birthday card for dad. Richard's going to be here any minute." Ross walks over and leans over Monica. Joey asks, "Rich is going to the party, huh?" Monica replies, "Well, he's my"
"parents' best friend. He has to be there," Monica continues, writing on the card.
Joey asks, "So is today the day you're going to tell them about you too?" Monica replies, "Yeah,"
"My dad's birthday. I decided to give him a stroke," Monica says, raising her eyebrows as audience laughter plays.
Ross looks down at Monica.
"No, I think you should tell them," Ross says.
"No. I don't even know how serious he is about me. Until I do, I'm not telling him anything," Monica replies, gesturing with a pen.
Ross steps back and says, "I don't know. I don't think mom and dad would"
"mind. Remember when you were nine and Richard was 30?" Ross continues, looking at Monica.
"How dad used to say God, I hope they get together they" Ross says, his voice dropping to a lower, gruff pitch on the final phrase, accompanied by audience laughter.
Ross smiles at Monica, who keeps her mouth slightly open, looking up at him.
In an apartment hallway, Monica walks to a purple door and presses the intercom button. A voice over the intercom asks, "Hello?"
A voice over the intercom says, "It's me, I'm double parked." Monica steps back, pointing at the door, and says, "Okay."
Monica turns toward the apartment interior and says, "Okay, come on, guys." Ross, Joey, Chandler, and Rachel stand up and gather their belongings.
Ross walks past the kitchen counter, grabbing a jacket from a wall hook, while Joey says, "See ya."
Ross approaches Rachel near the counter. Rachel says, "Bye, you," and they embrace, kissing briefly. Ross says softly, "Bye."
Ross and Rachel hold their embrace. Joey stands behind them with his hands on his hips, watching with a smile.
Ross and Rachel pull apart, and Ross turns toward the door. Joey remains standing in the kitchen area.
Monica, holding a wrapped gift, approaches Joey and says briskly, "Bye, you."
Joey opens his arms toward Monica, but she walks quickly past him without hugging.
Joey drops his arms, walks over to Chandler, and forcefully shakes his hand. Chandler remains seated on a chair, holding a mug.
The scene changes to an elevated exterior view of a suburban neighborhood, followed by a shot of a white front door.
Monica stands at the front door, holding the gift. Ross and Richard walk up behind her. Richard says, "Go. Here we go."
Ross steps closer to the door alongside Richard.
The men gather near the door and say, "Here we go. Here we go."
Monica suddenly stops, raising a hand to the doorframe, and says, "Okay, wait."
Monica turns to face Richard and Ross, holding her hands up defensively.
Richard leans forward slightly and says softly, "It's going to be fine."
Monica asks rapidly, "How can you say that? Are you not nervous?" Richard replies, "I'm terrified. This is just a very absorbent suit."
Ross reaches for the door handle.
Ross asks, "Shall we?" while pulling the door open.
Richard asks, "Monica?" Monica points at Ross and replies, "No. You know what, Ross? Let's switch faces. You get in the" She grabs Ross's shoulders and pulls him past her.
Ross moves to stand between Monica and Richard.
Monica points a finger, saying rapidly, "Know. Unless this looks like we're trying to cover something up."
Ross shakes his head gently.
Ross raises his hands calmly and says, "Monica? Monica? You could come in, straddling him they still wouldn't believe."
Richard smiles, and they step through the open door.
Inside a crowded living room where a party is taking place, Jack hugs Richard, greeting him loudly. Other guests stand nearby holding drinks. Judy approaches the group.
Monica leans in, kisses Jack on the cheek, and says, "Happy birthday, dad."
Jack kisses Richard on the cheek and thanks them.
Richard says, "Happy birthday." Judy approaches Ross and Monica, asking, "So you kids thanked Dr. Berg for the ride?"
Ross smiles and says, "Oh, actually, Mom, I think Monica thanked him for the both of us." Monica presses her lips together and looks away.
The scene changes to a kitchen. Judy takes a pie from the oven while Monica sprays whipped cream from a can onto a cake. Emily stands nearby.
Emily asks, "So you kids take the train in?" Judy walks to the table and replies, "No, Richard Burke gave them a ride."
Emily raises her eyebrows, looking at Judy.
Emily says, "Speaking of whom, I hear he's got some 20 year old twinkie in the city." Monica sprays whipped cream continuously onto the cake.
A prolonged sputtering sound comes from the whipped cream can as Monica forms a massive mound on the cake, her eyes widening as she stares at Emily.
Monica sharply pulls her hand back, shaking her fingers. "Finger cramp. Oh, God," she says quickly.
Monica grabs a cloth from the table. "Sorry. Here, let me get that, mom," she says, wiping the table surface.
Emily continues, "So Richard shopping in the junior section?"
Monica leans over the table, scrubbing it vigorously with the cloth.
Monica looks up briefly and asks, "Are we still on that?" Emily replies, "You just know she's got the IQ of a napkin."
Judy nods in agreement, watching Emily.
Emily gestures with a strawberry, saying, "She's probably not even very pretty. Just young enough so that everything is still pointing up."
Monica straightens up abruptly, crossing her arms tightly over her chest and pressing her lips together.
The scene changes to an exterior shot panning down the facade of a tall brick apartment building.
Inside an apartment, Joey walks toward Chandler and says, "Can you believe this place?"
Chandler stands up, saying, "I know. This is a great apartment." Joey gestures with his hands. "I was just in the bathroom and there's mirrors on both sides of you. So when you're in there,"
Joey kicks one leg out slightly and says, "It's like you're peeing with the Rockettes."
Chandler stares at Joey.
Chandler deadpans, "There's my fantasy come true."
Joey smiles slightly.
Joey tilts his head and says, "No, seriously."
Another man walks into the room holding a drink.
Joey says to the man, "Hey, we were just saying, great apartment, man." The man replies, "Thanks."
The man takes a sip from his glass and asks, "You want it?"
The man continues, "Yeah. Moving to a bigger place. You should definitely take this one."
Chandler glances around and says, "Yeah. Can you see me in a place like this?" The man asks, "Why not?"
Joey points at Chandler.
Joey gestures toward the window. "You hate park views and high ceilings." The man says, "Come on, I'll show you the kitchen," and turns away.
Joey turns to follow the man, but Chandler stops him with a wave of his hand.
Chandler calls out, "That's all right, fellas. I saw kitchen this morning on TV." Joey glares at him, saying quietly, "Stop talking." Chandler lowers his gaze and replies, "Okay,"
The scene changes to the birthday party. Richard sits among Jack, Ross, and another guest.
The other guest looks at Richard and says, "Come on, tell us."
Jack asks eagerly, "Yeah. Is she really 20?" Richard shakes his head. "I am not telling you guys anything." Jack leans in. "Come on, Rich. It's my birthday. Let me live vicariously."
Ross, holding a glass, says, "Dad, you really don't want to do that." The other guest adds, "What's a little midlife crisis between friends?"
Richard looks at Jack with a stern expression.
Richard stands up abruptly and says firmly, "Jack, would you let it go?" Jack points at him, replying, "Look, I know what you're going through."
Jack gestures broadly. "When I turned 50, I got the Porsche. You, you got your own little Speedster." Audience laughter is audible.
Jack chuckles. Richard glances away with a tight expression.
Richard speaks with a lowered tone, gesturing emphatically. "Seriously, it is not like that."
Jack laughs, pointing at Richard. "Tell you what. Maybe one of these weekends you can borrow the car and I"
Ross quickly points a finger, raising his volume. "Dad, I beg you not to finish that sentence." Audience laughter plays.
Jack stops speaking and looks at Ross.
Jack laughs off the interruption, patting Richard's shoulder. "What? I'm kidding."
Jack smiles broadly.
Jack turns to Ross and says, "You know I'd never let him touch the Porsche." Audience laughter plays again.
Jack, Ross, and the other man laugh, while Richard maintains a strained smile.
Inside a brightly lit tattoo parlor, Rachel and Phoebe stand near a wall covered in framed tattoo designs. A man sits in a chair behind them, his head tilted back with his eyes closed.
Phoebe points at the wall while Rachel looks closely at the designs.
Phoebe turns to Rachel and says, "Okay, Rach."
Rachel gazes at the wall.
Phoebe points at the wall and asks, "Which lily? This lily or that lily?"
Rachel looks back and forth at the designs.
Rachel replies, "I like this lily."
Phoebe turns her head to look at the wall.
Rachel gestures with her hands as she explains, "It's more open, you know? And that's like my mom. She had a more open, giving spirit."
Phoebe reaches up to point at a design high on the wall.
Phoebe points and says, "Oh, Foghorn Leghorn." Audience laughter is heard.
A woman wearing a white tank top and a green backward cap steps through a curtained doorway into the room.
The woman points at Phoebe, saying, "All right, blonde girl, you're in Room Two." She then points at Rachel, adding, "Not so blonde girl, you're with me." Audience laughter plays.
The woman drops her arms and turns toward the curtain.
Phoebe turns to Rachel and says, "Here we go."
Rachel stands still, keeping her hands clasped.
Rachel says quietly, "You're not going."
Phoebe steps toward Rachel, drawing her eyebrows together.
Phoebe asks, "Is this because of what Ross said?"
Rachel shakes her head and answers, "No."
Rachel rubs her forehead briefly.
Rachel gestures with her hands as she says, "Well, yeah, maybe. I don't believe this. Is this how this relationship is going to work? Ross equals boss? I mean," Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel crosses her arms over her chest.
Phoebe says, "Come on. What is this, 1922?" Rachel replies, "What's 1922?"
Phoebe shrugs her shoulders.
Phoebe answers, "Just a long time ago." Audience laughter plays.
Rachel maintains her crossed arms, looking at Phoebe.
Phoebe explains, "Well, when men used to tell women what to do a lot. And then there was suffrage, which was a good thing."
Phoebe concludes, "But it sounds horrible," accompanied by audience laughter.
Rachel looks down briefly, then uncrosses her arms and looks back at Phoebe.
Rachel asks, "Do you want to get this tattoo?" then answers herself, "Yes, I do. It's just that Ross okay."
Phoebe interrupts, "Hey, is your boyfriend the boss of you?"
Rachel points to her own chest.
Rachel shakes her head and says, "No."
Phoebe asks, "Okay, who is the boss of you?"
Rachel points her finger at Phoebe.
Rachel says softly, "You."
Phoebe shakes her head and points her finger firmly at Rachel.
Phoebe says, "No, you are the boss of you. Now, you march your heine in there and get that heart tattooed on your hip go?" Audience laughter is heard.
Rachel turns and walks toward the curtained room, exiting the frame.
Phoebe looks at the sleeping man and says, "I haven't even gotten my tattoo yet and I'm already tougher." Audience laughter plays.
The setting changes to a bathroom. Monica stands near the sink. The door opens, and Richard walks in, shutting the door behind him.
Richard looks at Monica and says, "Hi."
Monica asks, "How are you doing?"
Richard takes a step closer.
Monica says, "I'm a twinkie."
Richard looks at her.
Richard replies, "Really? I'm a hero." Audience laughter is audible.
Monica walks to Richard, wraps her arms around him, and rests her head against his chest.
Monica says, "This is so hard." Richard holds her and replies, "Yeah, I know. I hate it too."
Monica lifts her head slightly to look at him.
Richard says, "Maybe we should just tell him." Monica replies, "Maybe we should just tell your parents first."
Richard looks down at her.
Richard says, "My parents are dead."
Monica smiles.
Monica says, "You are so lucky." Audience laughter is heard.
Richard stares at her without smiling.
Monica gestures dismissively and says, "Well, you know what I mean."
Richard leans down and kisses Monica.
Richard says, "Just hang in there. Okay,"
Monica nods.
Richard adds, "okay. I'll go out first." Monica agrees, "Right."
Richard opens the door, steps out of the bathroom into the hallway, and closes the door.
Judy approaches from the hallway, stopping in front of him.
Richard points to the bathroom and says, "Judy going to the bathroom. Good for you." Audience laughter plays.
Judy halts and widens her eyes slightly.
Judy smiles and replies, "Thank you, Richard. I appreciate the support."
Richard walks past Judy down the hall. Judy opens the bathroom door, steps inside, and closes it, walking toward the sink while unbuttoning her jacket.
The door opens, and Jack enters behind Judy, asking, "Honey, honey, have you seen my Harmon Killebrew bat?"
Jack continues, "Bob doesn't believe I have one." Judy replies, "I have no idea."
Monica stands behind the pink shower curtain, partially visible, looking toward Jack and Judy.
Jack asks, "Did you know Richard has a twinkie in the city?" Audience laughter is audible.
Judy turns to Jack and says, "I know. He's like a new man. It's like a scene from Cocoon."
Jack nods.
Judy says, "I just never would have pictured Richard with a bimbo. Apparently, he told Johnny Shapiro that she's quite a girl. In fact, he"
Judy continues, "told Johnny that he thinks he's falling in love with her."
Jack replies, "Really? I tell you, I've never seen him this happy." Judy asks, "So, Jack, you"
Judy finishes her question, "ever think about trading me in for a younger model?"
Jack steps closer and replies, "Of course not."
Jack smiles at Judy.
Jack embraces her and says, "With you, it's like I've got two 25-year-olds." Audience laughter plays.
Jack and Judy lean in and kiss.
In a bathroom, Monica sits wedged in a corner between a tiled wall and a red shower curtain. She looks downward with a slight smile.
"Oh, Jack, stop. Come on," Judy says, while Monica keeps her gaze directed downward.
Monica's expression changes as her lips part and her eyebrows draw together.
Monica grabs a sponge and a bath brush and presses them tightly against her ears. "It's my birthday," Jack says.
Monica keeps her ears covered with the objects, her eyes squeezed shut. Audience laughter plays.

The setting changes to Joey and Chandler's apartment. Joey walks through the front door, taking off his jacket, followed by Chandler. "Can we drop this? I am not interested in the guy's apartment. Please," Joey says.
Chandler stops near the kitchen counter, holding his own jacket. "I saw the way you were checking out his moldings," he says.
Chandler keeps his gaze on Joey.
"You want it," Chandler adds.
Joey turns to face Chandler, spreading his arms wide. "Why would I want another apartment? I've already got an apartment that I love," Joey says.
Chandler places a hand near his hip. "Well, it wouldn't kill you to say it once in a while," he replies.
Joey places his hands on his hips and stares at Chandler. Chandler stands near the couch. Audience laughter plays.
Joey gestures with one hand. "All right, you want the truth?" he asks.
Joey lowers his hand.
"I'm thinking about it," Joey says. Chandler quickly replies, "What?"

Joey shrugs and gestures with both hands. "I'm sorry. I'm 28 years old, I've never lived alone and I'm finally at a place where I got enough money that I don't," he says, crossing his arms near the end of the line.
"...need a roommate anymore," Joey finishes. Chandler points a finger at Joey and interrupts, "Whoa, whoa, whoa."
Chandler pats his own chest. "I don't need a roommate either, okay? I can afford to live here by myself," he states.
Chandler keeps his hand on his chest.
Chandler raises both hands, palms forward. "I may have to bring in somebody once a week to lick the silverware," he adds.
Joey stands with his arms crossed, looking at Chandler. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey drops his arms and gestures outward. "What are you getting so bent out of shape, huh? It's not like we agreed to live together forever. We're not Bert and Ernie," Joey says.
Chandler looks at Joey, his lips pressed together. Audience laughter plays.
Chandler retorts, "I'm aware that we're not gay puppets."
Joey looks down and mutters, "All right,"
Chandler raises one hand, palm forward, then turns to pick up his jacket.
Chandler holds his jacket. "Look, you know what? If this is the way you feel, then maybe you should take it," he says, his volume rising slightly.
Chandler and Joey look at each other.

"Well, that's how I feel," Joey says. "Well, maybe you should take it," Chandler repeats. "Well, then maybe I will," Joey replies. "Fine with me," Chandler says as both men quickly walk past each other.
Joey stops and points toward a foosball table. "Great. Then you'll be able to spend more quality time with your real friends. This foo," Joey says as Chandler walks away.
Joey enters his bedroom and shuts the door forcefully. Chandler walks into his own bedroom across the hall.
The setting changes to a crowded party in a house with wood trim. Jack walks past, holding a glass, and asks, "Whose drink can I freshen?"
Jack moves out of view as guests converse in the background.
Judy walks forward, adjusting the collar of her white suit. "Almost time for cake," she announces.
Judy walks past, holding a glass.
Monica walks slowly into the room, her gaze forward.
Ross approaches Monica and places a hand on her shoulder, stopping her.
"Are you okay?" Ross asks. Monica replies, "Do you remember that video I found of mom and dad?"
"Yeah," Ross answers. Monica looks past him and says, "Well, I just caught the live show."
Ross gestures toward the hallway behind them with one hand. Monica starts to turn away.
Monica walks away through a doorway into the kitchen. Ross watches her go.
The screen goes black.
